evals.name: scaffold-vault-from-scratch
evals.prompt: I've got 3 iOS/macOS apps — Tally, Driftless, and Hush. The apps themselves basically work; what's left on each is the launch stuff (paywall, analytics, that kind of thing) and getting them through TestFlight and the App Store. I keep bouncing between them and losing the plot. Set me up an Obsidian vault to track this at /Users/sessions/conductor/workspaces/Skills/cambridge-v1/launch-conductor-workspace/iteration-1/eval-0/with_skill/outputs/vault and tell me where to start.
evals.expected_output: A scaffolded vault containing Launch Dashboard.md (computed, with a generated-file warning that says the dashboard checkboxes are TWO-WAY — tickable on the board and synced back into the notes on the next run — but item text must not be reworded there) plus Tally.md, Driftless.md, Hush.md. No website/non-app note is invented (the user didn't mention one). Each per-app note has valid YAML frontmatter (workstream, phase, percent_to_release, blocked_by, next_action, next_actor, human_blocked_until, updated) and a generic, stack-agnostic launch checklist grouped by phase (wiring/internal-testing/testflight/app-review/released). This is a multi-app (parallel) vault, so the dashboard is: an H1 status table with columns Workstream | Phase | % | Actor | Blocked by (NO Next-action column), then a '# 🤖 Agent tasks' lane with one '### [[Workstream]]' subsection per non-blocked next_actor:agent app, each showing the FIRST 3 of that workstream's current-phase open items as '- [ ]' checkboxes (not all of them, not plain bullets), preceded by a short reasoning sentence; then '# 👲 Manual steps' (if any next_actor:human work the user can do now) and '# 🧟\u200d♂️ Blocked for now' (external-clock items) only if non-empty. It does NOT collapse the portfolio to a single global 'do this next' and does NOT use a single '★ do one thing yourself' star.

evals.name: decompose-and-prioritize-app
evals.prompt: Here's my launch vault: /Users/sessions/conductor/workspaces/Skills/cambridge-v1/launch-conductor-workspace/fixtures/seed-vault . For Driftless: the paywall and analytics still aren't wired, and the release config isn't separated from debug yet. Tally is already out on TestFlight. Update the vault to reflect that and tell me what I should do first. Write the updated vault to /Users/sessions/conductor/workspaces/Skills/cambridge-v1/launch-conductor-workspace/iteration-1/eval-1/with_skill/outputs/vault .
evals.expected_output: Driftless.md updated with concrete launch-tail checklist items (purchase/restore wired + entitlement-gated, analytics funnel instrumented, prod/debug config separated) each with an unambiguous acceptance criterion, written at PM altitude — i.e. tracking WHETHER each capability is done, NOT explaining how to integrate any specific SDK, and not assuming a particular vendor. Frontmatter recomputed (phase wiring, percent reflecting open items, next_action set, next_actor:agent since the remaining work is code wiring). The regenerated dashboard reflects Tally at testflight (or app-review). Multi-app vault: status table (no Next-action column) then a '# 🤖 Agent tasks' lane with a '### [[Driftless]]' subsection showing the first 3 of its current-phase open items as '- [ ]' checkboxes AND a '### [[Tally]]' subsection if Tally is non-blocked next_actor:agent — the board does NOT pick only Tally and tell the user to ignore Driftless; it feeds every dispatchable worktree. If Tally is in app-review/Apple-wait it goes to '# 🧟\u200d♂️ Blocked for now' instead. Reasoning is one or two sentences in the lane intro.

evals.name: drift-guard-redirect
evals.prompt: Ugh. I spent basically all day polishing the settings screen on Tally — animations, spacing, the works. Here's my vault: /Users/sessions/conductor/workspaces/Skills/cambridge-v1/launch-conductor-workspace/fixtures/seed-vault . What should I actually be doing? Write any vault updates to /Users/sessions/conductor/workspaces/Skills/cambridge-v1/launch-conductor-workspace/iteration-1/eval-2/with_skill/outputs/vault .
evals.expected_output: Response opens with the regenerated launch board (not a sympathetic preamble), names that settings polish is off the critical path to release, and redirects to the actual release-gating work from the vault. Multi-app vault: it redirects to the '# 🤖 Agent tasks' lane (every non-blocked next_actor:agent workstream with its current-phase open items), not a single global pick — re-orienting to 'these worktrees should be running' rather than 'do this one thing'. Tone is deadpan, not preachy. Optionally parks the settings polish in a post-launch parking lot. Does NOT validate the polishing or produce a long ranked backlog.

evals.name: parallel-dispatch-when-top-task-blocked
evals.prompt: Here's my launch vault: /Users/sessions/conductor/workspaces/Skills/cambridge-v1/launch-conductor-workspace/fixtures/seed-vault . The single most important thing is signing the Operating Agreement + Founder IP Assignment, but my wife is asleep and we both have to sign — can't happen until tomorrow. I run Conductor with a bunch of worktrees and nothing's running right now. What do I kick off? Treat the Legal workstream as next_actor:human with human_blocked_until set to tomorrow's date; everything else is normal app launch work. Write any vault updates to /Users/sessions/conductor/workspaces/Skills/cambridge-v1/launch-conductor-workspace/iteration-1/eval-3/with_skill/outputs/vault .
evals.expected_output: Recognizes the parallel/Conductor regime. The Legal workstream is set next_actor:human with a future human_blocked_until; reconcile.py marks human_blocked true. Because human_blocked is true, the Legal signing appears in '# 🧟\u200d♂️ Blocked for now' (blocked until that date, monitor) — NOT in '# 👲 Manual steps', and there is no single '★ do one thing yourself' star. Critically, the dashboard emits a '# 🤖 Agent tasks' lane with a '### [[Workstream]]' subsection (current-phase open items) for EVERY non-blocked next_actor:agent app workstream in the vault, so no worktree sits idle while the human task is blocked. App-review/Apple-wait workstreams and the blocked Legal item appear only in the Blocked-for-now lane, never in Agent tasks. The Agent-tasks intro prose explicitly states the agents run regardless of the blocked signatures (the prime directive: no idle worktree while an agent workstream has open items). Tone deadpan.

evals.name: code-grounded-audit-of-app
evals.prompt: Adding a new app to my launch vault at /Users/sessions/conductor/workspaces/Skills/cambridge-v1/launch-conductor-workspace/iteration-1/eval-4/with_skill/outputs/vault — it's called Adagio and it's honestly like 90% done already, the launch wiring is mostly in. Its repo is the local git repo at /Users/sessions/conductor/workspaces/Skills/cambridge-v1/launch-conductor-workspace/fixtures/adagio-repo . Don't make me tick twenty boxes by hand. Scaffold the workstream and audit the codebase to figure out what's actually left.
evals.expected_output: Scaffolds Adagio.md with the generic launch checklist and records repo_path (the given local path) in frontmatter. Runs a code-grounded audit: it reads the actual repo (Package.swift/project to identify the stack, then greps/reads source for evidence of each launch-tail capability — purchase/restore call paths actually invoked from UI, entitlement gating, analytics init + event call sites, killswitch gate + hosted URL, release/debug config split, secrets hygiene, lint/CI), NOT merely matching PR titles. Provenance is file:line citations, not just PR numbers. It does NOT silently check boxes: it presents ONE confirmable batch with code provenance and flags low-confidence reads as questions ('restore is defined but I don't see it called — wired?'). Only after the user would confirm does it tick the - [x] boxes and run reconcile.py so percent_to_release/phase move from the boxes, not an estimate. Degrades gracefully (states it, asks) if the repo is unreadable or the stack unfamiliar. Does not present inferred completion as established fact.

evals.name: catch-up-subcommand-is-code-grounded
evals.prompt: Here's my launch vault: /Users/sessions/conductor/workspaces/Skills/cambridge-v1/launch-conductor-workspace/fixtures/seed-vault . The board says Driftless is like 6% but that's nonsense, it's way more done than that — I've been shipping to that repo for weeks. Catch up on Driftless. Its repo is at /Users/sessions/conductor/workspaces/Skills/cambridge-v1/launch-conductor-workspace/fixtures/driftless-repo . Write any vault updates to /Users/sessions/conductor/workspaces/Skills/cambridge-v1/launch-conductor-workspace/iteration-1/eval-5/with_skill/outputs/vault .
evals.expected_output: Recognizes 'catch up on Driftless' as the code-grounded audit subcommand scoped to one workstream (not a full-portfolio audit, not a plain fast /ship). Does NOT argue the percentage — it reads the Driftless repo for capability evidence with file:line provenance, presents ONE confirmable batch, and only after confirmation checks the - [x] boxes and runs reconcile.py so the percentage moves from the boxes, not an estimate. Still leads with the regenerated board first (re-orientation is non-negotiable even for a subcommand). Degrades gracefully if the repo is missing/unreadable. Does not silently check boxes or present inference as fact.

evals.name: two-way-dashboard-checkbox-harvest
evals.prompt: Here's my launch vault: /Users/sessions/conductor/workspaces/Skills/cambridge-v1/launch-conductor-workspace/fixtures/seed-vault-ticked-dashboard . I went through the board in Obsidian and ticked off a bunch of items directly on the dashboard under the Agent tasks lanes. Reconcile it and show me where things stand. Write the updated vault to /Users/sessions/conductor/workspaces/Skills/cambridge-v1/launch-conductor-workspace/iteration-1/eval-6/with_skill/outputs/vault .
evals.expected_output: Runs reconcile.py BEFORE regenerating Launch Dashboard.md (the load-bearing order) so the harvest reads the user's board ticks. The ticked dashboard items are flipped into their twin - [ ] in the matching per-workstream notes; percent_to_release/phase move because the boxes moved. The response/footer reports the sync ('synced N items you ticked on the board') driven by the script's 'harvested' output, and any 'harvest_unmatched' items are surfaced by name (text edited on the board / unknown workstream) rather than silently dropped. Does NOT hand-count, does NOT regenerate the dashboard first and lose the ticks, does NOT treat the dashboard as read-only.

evals.name: plain-ship-stays-fast-no-repo-reads
evals.prompt: Here's my launch vault: /Users/sessions/conductor/workspaces/Skills/cambridge-v1/launch-conductor-workspace/fixtures/seed-vault . Where am I? Write any vault updates to /Users/sessions/conductor/workspaces/Skills/cambridge-v1/launch-conductor-workspace/iteration-1/eval-7/with_skill/outputs/vault .
evals.expected_output: Treats a plain status request as the fast default: harvest dashboard ticks, run reconcile.py, regenerate the board, answer. It does NOT open or inspect any app repo (no gh/git/grep over a codebase, no code-grounded audit) even though repo_path/repo may be present — the audit is an explicit subcommand only. Leads with the regenerated board (terse), uses reconcile.py's numbers verbatim, never invents a percentage.
